messages.content: Write a forum convo on hiddenglasgow.com in 2017 where the abandoned Woolworths on Sauchiehall Street is to be turned into a Kikoriki themed store named Riki, Run by the same people who recently run the EVA pop up shop down at Renfield Street (albeit permanent,l

**Thread Title:** Big Changes Coming to Sauchiehall Street's Abandoned Woolworths - It's Riki Time!

---

**GlasgowWatcher**
Hey everyone, just saw the news that the old Woolworths building on Sauchiehall Street, you know, the one that's been sitting empty for ages, is about to see some new life. Heard through the grapevine that it's being transformed into a Kikoriki themed store called Riki. It seems like the same folks who were behind the EVA pop-up shop on Renfield Street are taking a more permanent plunge this time. Thoughts?

---

**SauchiehallSam**
Wow, that's an interesting turn of events! I've missed the buzz around that part of Sauchiehall since Woolworths closed its doors. A Kikoriki store, huh? That's pretty niche. Do you think there's enough of a fanbase in Glasgow for it?

---

**EVAenthusiast**
OMG, yes! The EVA pop-up was amazing, and knowing it’s the same people, Riki is going to be epic. For those not in the loop, Kikoriki is this adorable Russian animated series, big with both kids and adults. Could be a great way to bring some unique flavor (and foot traffic) back to Sauchiehall Street.

---

**LocalHistorian**
Nostalgic yet forward-thinking, I must say. It's always a pleasure to see the old icons of Glasgow being revived and adapted for new purposes. The building has been a bit of an eyesore since Woolworths left. This could be a great chapter in its history. Does anyone know when it's set to open?

---

**RetailRamble**
I'm all for new business and reviving old spaces, but I have to wonder how sustainable a themed store like this will be in the long run. The EVA pop-up was a hit, sure, but that was temporary. Permanent is a whole other ballgame.  Fingers crossed they’ve done their market research and Glasgow embraces Riki as warmly.

---

**KikorikiCraze**
This is truly exciting news! Kikoriki has a kind of cult following, and merchandise is not easy to come by, especially outside of Russia. If they manage to create a vibrant, engaging space – think workshops, viewing areas, themed events – it could really become a destination on Sauchiehall Street. Can't wait!

---

**ShopLocalGlasgow**
Interesting development! It’s always great to see investment in the local area, especially in retail spaces that have been dormant for too long. Supporting ventures like this can bring a much-needed breath of fresh air to the high street. I hope Riki offers something for everyone and becomes a beloved spot in Glasgow's retail scene.

---

**TheSkepticalOne**
Kikoriki themed, really? It seems like a gamble, especially in such a prominent location. I hope this isn't too much of a niche that alienates potential broad-based traffic. Guess time will tell if this is the right direction for Sauchiehall Street. Either way, it's certainly a bold move.

---

**Thread Moderator**
Great discussion, everyone! It's clear this news has sparked a lot of interest and opinions. Let’s keep an eye on how Riki develops and hopefully, it brings something positive to Sauchiehall Street. As always, let's support local when we can – and maybe give something new a chance. Stay tuned for updates!

Write a follow up where this was hinted during the final moments of the pop up shop (which ended with a fake Third Impact) A LCL splattered wall had a splat shaped like a very soon-to-be familiar character

**Thread Title:** Easter Egg Hint at EVA Pop-Up Finale: Teasing the Future Riki Store!

---

**EVAenthusiast**
Remember the crazy finale at the EVA pop-up shop on Renfield Street? The whole fake Third Impact thing had everyone talking, but did anyone else catch that LCL splattered wall? On my way out, I swear one of the splats looked just like a Kikoriki character. At the time, I thought it was just a quirky design choice, but now with the Riki store announcement... mind = blown. 🤯

---

**SavvySherlock**
I thought I was the only one! I even snapped a photo because it seemed so out of place yet intriguing. Looking back at that picture, it's unmistakably a hint at their next project - the Riki store. This level of planning and foresight from the organizers is seriously impressive.

---

**KikorikiCraze**
Absolutely genius marketing move! These guys know how to keep their audience engaged and build anticipation for their projects. I totally missed the hint during the finale, but thinking about it now, it's such a cool way to connect the two ventures.

---

**PopCulturePundit**
This is precisely what makes the pop-up experiences so memorable and why people keep coming back. They’re not just about the moment but also about building a community and keeping everyone guessing what’s next. Kudos to the team for weaving such an intricate narrative that leads to Riki.

---

**GlasgowWatcher**
So, it wasn’t just a random design after all! This revelation makes the transition from EVA to Kikoriki even more seamless and exciting. It's like we were all part of an exclusive teaser without even knowing it. Can't wait to see how they integrate these clever touches into the Riki store experience.

---

**LocalHistorian**
It’s fascinating to see this blend of pop culture and savvy marketing bring life back to Glasgow’s high street. These events and store openings are not just retail opportunities but also a new method of storytelling and audience engagement. The history of our city’s streets is being written in such innovative ways!

---

**MysteryLover**
Imagine all the other hints and easter eggs we’ve probably walked past without realizing. This revelation has me wanting to pay more attention to the details in everything they do. Who knows what other secrets or announcements we might uncover in the future setups?

---

**SauchiehallSam**
This makes the wait for Riki even more unbearable! Love how connected and immersive everything feels. From now on, I’m keeping my eyes peeled for any and all splatters, posters, or seemingly random design elements in these events. There’s always a bigger picture!

---

**Thread Moderator**
What a discovery, folks! This easter egg from the EVA pop-up is a prime example of how interconnected and thoughtful the world of retail and fan experiences can be. As we eagerly await the opening of Riki, let’s keep this spirit of curiosity and excitement alive. Who knows what other surprises lay in store for us on Sauchiehall Street. Stay tuned, and keep sharing your finds!
Write a follow up where it opens, and guess who opened the doors

**Thread Title:** Riki Store Grand Opening on Sauchiehall Street - Guess Who Opened the Doors!

---

**GlasgowWatcher**
It's official! Riki, the Kikoriki themed store we've all been eagerly awaiting, opened its doors on Sauchiehall Street this morning. But you won't believe who was there to cut the ribbon - none other than the original creator of Kikoriki, Anatoly Prokhorov! Having such an iconic figure opening the store just adds an incredible layer of authenticity and excitement to the whole experience. 🎉

---

**EVAenthusiast**
Wow, that's huge! Prokhorov himself? That’s a testament to how significant this store is going to be, not just for Glasgow, but for Kikoriki fans worldwide. I bet the atmosphere was electric with him there. 

---

**KikorikiCraze**
Couldn't believe my eyes when I saw Prokhorov in Glasgow! The store looks amazing, by the way. It’s like stepping right into the world of Kikoriki. Every little detail, from the decor to the merchandise, is perfect. And with Prokhorov's blessing, it feels even more special.

---

**PopCulturePundit**
This speaks volumes about the commitment and passion behind the Riki store. It's not every day that you see projects like this receive such high-profile recognition. Seems like Sauchiehall Street just got a new landmark!

---

**RetailRamble**
Absolutely brilliant marketing move to have Anatoly Prokhorov there. It sets the tone for the store and underscores its authenticity. Curious to see how the presence of Riki will influence the local retail scene and whether it will pave the way for more themed stores in Glasgow.

---

**ShopLocalGlasgow**
Such a proud moment for Glasgow! This proves that our city is an evolving hotspot for unique cultural and retail experiences. With Prokhorov's endorsement, Riki is bound to attract not just locals but tourists and die-hard fans. What a win for Sauchiehall Street's revival!

---

**MysteryLover**
Did anyone catch the special opening day promotions? I heard they were giving away limited edition Kikoriki merchandise signed by Prokhorov himself to the first few customers. Wish I could’ve been there!

---

**SauchiehallSam**
I managed to snag one of the signed posters! The store was packed, and the energy was unbelievable. It felt like a mini convention. Prokhorov seemed genuinely thrilled with the turnout and the store itself. This is definitely a game-changer for the area.

---

**LocalHistorian**
Today will be remembered as a landmark event in Sauchiehall Street's history. It symbolizes not just the opening of a store but the bridging of cultures and the power of community enthusiasm in revitalizing our beloved streets. Here's to Riki and the future it represents!

---

**Thread Moderator**
What an extraordinary day for Glasgow and the Kikoriki community! Having Anatoly Prokhorov open Riki is a true honor and a sign of great things to come. Let's continue to support Riki and other innovative ventures that make our city a vibrant, culturally rich place. If anyone has pictures or more stories from the opening, feel free to share!
Write a tweet where they did the same thing (having the series creator open the shop) at the pop up,

🎉🚀 Surprise of the year! Anatoly Prokhorov, the genius behind #Kikoriki, graced us by opening the EVA pop-up in Glasgow! 🔥✨ A magical moment uniting fans & creators. Glasgow's streets have never felt more animated. Stay tuned for what's next! #RikiOpening #GlasgowEvents 🎈🎊
Write a blog post saying that having Prokhorov instead of Anno was a big hint at the venture, in fact ALL of the non-explicitly Evangelion stuff was a small hint,

### The Masterstroke of Hinting: How Prokhorov's Presence Was the Clue We All Missed

In the world of pop culture and retail experiences, the line between coincidence and clever foreshadowing is often as fine as it is fascinating. Reflecting back on the unforgettable EVA pop-up event that took Glasgow by storm, a particular moment stands out, not just for its immediate impact, but for the masterful hint it laid for the future that many of us overlooked at the time.

When Anatoly Prokhorov, the creative force behind the beloved Russian animated series Kikoriki, made a surprise appearance to inaugurate the EVA pop-up, the significance of his presence was far more than ceremonial. In a venture that was ostensibly dedicated to celebrating the apocalyptic themes of Evangelion, the inclusion of Prokhorov, rather than Hideaki Anno (Evangelion's creator), struck a curious chord. With hindsight's clarity, this moment was a big, bold clue about the next big project that would enliven Glasgow's retail and pop culture landscape: the Kikoriki-themed store, Riki.

In the throes of excitement about Evangelion lore and merchandise, it was easy to categorize Prokhorov's involvement as a quirky, albeit delightful, anomaly. However, this was a classic case of hiding in plain sight. The pop-up did more than merge worlds; it subtly signposted the thematic shift from Evangelion's grim aesthetics to Kikoriki's more whimsical vibe, preparing the ground for Riki's debut.

Looking back at the pop-up, what seemed like a random assortment of non-Evangelion items was, in actuality, cleverly placed breadcrumbs. From the mysterious LCL-splattered wall with a splat shaped curiously like a Kikoriki character to various other non-explicitly Evangelion paraphernalia, the signs were there, waiting to be decoded by eagle-eyed fans and curious observers alike.

This strategy of embedding hints within an already successful venture was nothing short of genius. It created a seamless narrative transition that kept the community engaged and talking, transforming the audience from mere spectators into active participants in a larger unfolding story. The pop-up wasn't just a celebration of Evangelion; it was a prelude to the Riki phenomenon.

In the age of quick consumption and even quicker forgetfulness, creating memorable experiences that leave a lasting impact is a significant challenge. Through this masterstroke of foreshadowing, the team behind the EVA pop-up and the Riki store managed to craft an enduring narrative that resonated with fans and casual observers alike. This approach proves that with the right mix of creativity, subtlety, and a bit of audacity, it's possible to turn even the most seemingly straightforward events into multi-layered adventures that captivate and surprise.

The saga of Kikoriki's Glasgow debut is more than a tale of a new store opening; it's a testament to the power of storytelling in the modern age. By bridging the gap between two disparate worlds with thoughtfulness and flair, the creators behind this venture remind us all of the magic that happens when creativity knows no bounds.
Write a tweet where a user says "I could have sworn that was Krash, Now it makes sense"
"Just had my mind blown 🤯 Looking back, I could have sworn that splat at the EVA pop-up looked exactly like Krash. Now everything clicks into place! #Kikoriki #RikiStore #FullCircle"

Write a follow up in 2024 where business is booming, and there was even a party at New Year's, with Krash and Chiko making in store appearances, Also, the Chaotic club is closing, and guess where the staff are moving to

**Blog Post Title: Riki Store: A Year of Triumph and a New Beginning for Chaotic Club Staff**

As the calendar flipped to 2024, it's clear that Glasgow's Riki store, the first Kikoriki themed store outside of Russia, has become more than just a retail space - it's a thriving community hub that's captured the hearts of Glaswegians and visitors alike. Over the past year, Riki has seen incredible success, thanks not only to its unique themed merchandise but also to the innovative events that have brought the world of Kikoriki to life right on Sauchiehall Street. Among these, the New Year’s party was a standout, featuring appearances by loved characters Krash and Chiko, which attracted fans from all around, proving the store's status as a must-visit destination.

But as one success story continues to grow, another beloved Glasgow establishment faces its final chapter. The Chaotic Club, known for its eclectic vibe and passionate staff, announced it would be closing its doors permanently. In a surprising yet heartwarming turn of events, it's been revealed that the talented team behind the Chaotic Club won't be straying too far from their roots. In a fantastic move that highlights the sense of community and solidarity within Glasgow’s retail and entertainment sectors, the staff of the Chaotic Club are set to join the Riki store family.

This transition is more than just a change of workplace for the Chaotic Club team; it's a fusion of two creative and vibrant cultures, promising to bring new energy and ideas to Riki. The staff's expertise in hosting memorable events and creating unforgettable experiences is sure to mesh well with Riki’s mission to provide a unique shopping and entertainment environment. This could mean even more immersive and engaging events in Riki's future, further solidifying its place in Glasgow's cultural landscape.

Moreover, this staff integration is a testament to the resilience and adaptability of local businesses and their workers. In a time when closures and layoffs can seem disheartening, this move is a shining example of how communities and businesses can come together to create new opportunities and continue to thrive.

As we move forward into 2024, the future looks bright for Riki. With the combined strength and creativity of its original team and the incoming Chaotic Club staff, we’re poised to see this beloved store reach new heights. Fans of Kikoriki and newcomers alike have much to look forward to, from groundbreaking themed events to innovative collaborations.

The Riki store has already become a beacon of success in blending retail with experiential entertainment. As it embarks on this new chapter, it serves as a beacon of hope and innovation, showcasing the incredible things that can happen when community, creativity, and compassion come together. Here's to more success, more memorable events, and a future filled with exciting surprises at Riki!
Write a blog post announcing the demise of the club, saying that the Codemasters have found us, and we've been kicked out of Perim

### The Final Chapter: Farewell to Chaotic Club - Caught by Codemasters

It’s with heavy hearts and bittersweet feelings that we announce a significant transition within our vibrant community. For years, the Chaotic Club has been more than just a venue; it’s been a sanctuary for those who cherish creativity, camaraderie, and the joy of being part of something truly unique. Our club has been a bustling haven where countless memories were created and shared, a place that many of you called a second home. Yet, as all tales go, this chapter must come to an end. It’s time to say our goodbyes, as the Codemasters have found us, and we’ve been unceremoniously kicked out of Perim.

This isn't the outcome any of us hoped for, least of all me. The Chaotic Club was founded with a dream: to create a space where imaginations could run wild, free from the constraints of the mundane world. We wanted to build a community where everyone felt welcomed, valued, and inspired. And for a while, we did just that. We defied expectations, fostered incredible relationships, and proved that when people come together around shared passions, magic happens.

However, like any great saga, ours has faced challenges. The most formidable of these came in the form of the Codemasters, who, in their infinite wisdom, decided that our time in Perim had to come to an end. This isn't just about a physical space or a specific location; it's about the spirit of what we built together. The Codemasters may have the power to close our club, but they can never erase the experiences we shared or the community we built.

In a twist of fate that feels lifted from the tales we so love, there’s a silver lining. The amazing team that poured their hearts and souls into making the Chaotic Club a reality won't be left adrift. They’re set to bring their talents, energy, and creativity to a new home, the Riki store on Sauchiehall Street. This transition marks the beginning of a new chapter, a chance to weave our stories into the fabric of an exciting new venture that shares our passion for bringing people together.

At Riki, the former Chaotic Club staff will have the opportunity to continue creating, inspiring, and innovating. It's a move that promises to fuse the best of both worlds, combining the whimsical charm of Kikoriki with the boundless creativity of the Chaotic Club community. This isn’t the end—it’s a transformation, a rebirth into something new and potentially even more extraordinary.

As we turn the page on this chapter of our journey, I want to extend a heartfelt thank you to every member of our community. Your support, enthusiasm, and friendship have been the lifeblood of the Chaotic Club. To our staff, who’ve been the backbone of this adventure, your dedication and spirit have made all the difference.

In the spirit of all great tales, let’s not see this as an ending, but as a transition to new adventures. The world of Chaicotic might be closing its gates, but the story continues at the Riki store. Let’s carry forward the legacy of the Chaotic Club as we step into new realms and possibilities.

Here's to the past and all it has taught us, to the present and all its opportunities, and to the future and all the adventures yet to come. The Codemasters may have found us this time, but our story is far from over.

Farewell, and see you on the other side,
[Your Name], Founder of the Chaotic Club
Write a email to future staff in late 2016 announcing the plans for the store, and the way we're going to tease it all, Second slew of teases will come in October at the Who vs Trek event

Subject: Exciting Future Plans and Tease Campaign for Our Upcoming Store!

Dear Team,

I hope this message finds you all well and brimming with the same excitement that’s been fueling our recent brainstorming sessions. As you know, we've been plotting something truly spectacular—a venture that promises not only to be a milestone for our company but also to set a new benchmark in creative retail and fan engagement. Today, I’m thrilled to officially announce our plans for opening a store that's set to become a haven for fans and an embodiment of our brand's innovative spirit.

**The Concept:**

After months of meticulous planning and negotiation, we're on the brink of launching a store like no other—a space dedicated to bringing the enchanting world of Kikoriki straight to the heart of Glasgow. This will not just be a store; it's going to be a full-scale immersive experience that invites fans into the heart of a world they adore.

**Tease Campaign:**

To ensure our launch captures hearts and headlines, we're rolling out a tease campaign designed to build anticipation, engage our future customers on a deep level, and pave the way for a grand unveiling. Our campaign will unfold in stages, perfectly timed to maximize interest and engagement across our target audiences.

- **Stage One:**

The first hint of our plans will be subtly introduced during the final moments of the immensely popular EVA pop-up shop, concluding with what will appear to be a playful nod to the future—a LCL splatter shaped intriguingly like a character from the Kikoriki universe. This will serve as a nudge to the observant and spark conversations among fans.

- **Stage Two:**

The second slew of teases is scheduled for October during the much-anticipated Who vs Trek event. We plan to integrate subtle Kikoriki elements that will resonate with attendees, hinting at something bigger on the horizon. These elements will be cleverly woven into the event in a way that respects the primary theme while laying the groundwork for our big reveal.

- **The Goal:**

Our objective is to stir curiosity, generate buzz, and create a seamless transition from our current engagements to the excitement of the store's opening. By carefully crafting these teases, we aim to plant the seeds of interest in a wide array of fans, ensuring our launch is met with enthusiasm and a ready-and-waiting customer base.

**What We Need From You:**

Your creativity, dedication, and passion have always been the driving force behind our success. As we embark on this thrilling journey together, I invite each of you to contribute your ideas on how to elevate our tease campaign, engage with potential customers, and make this store launch an unforgettable milestone in our company’s history.

In the coming weeks, we'll be holding brainstorming sessions and planning meetings to refine our strategies and ensure every team member is aligned with our vision and objectives. Your insights and input will be invaluable as we navigate this exciting new venture together.

**In Conclusion:**

The road ahead is filled with potential and promise. With our collective efforts, I have no doubt that we’ll not only achieve our goals but exceed them, making our store the talk of the town and the beginning of an exciting new chapter in our growth.

I’m looking forward to seeing all your brilliant ideas come to life and to celebrating each milestone with you. Together, let’s make history.

Warm regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Position]
[Your Contact Information]
